Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have a prompt which describes two parts Part 1 is Normative Process Behavior strict rules then Part 2 is Desiderata conditions Then it has an Event Log table of three cases each with timestamps The task is Identify normative rule violations and desiderata deviations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case ID 1 check if sequence strictly correct Application Received 2024 01 10 09 00 Credit Check 09 30 Risk Assessment 10 00 Loan Decision Approved at 11 00 Inform Applicant 13 00 Finalize Documentation 13 30 Close Case 14 00 There is no violation of strict order or normative rules

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also check desiderata Credit Check within 24 hours after application received in case 1 check Application Received at 09 00 and Credit Check occurred at 09 30 on same day So that is well within recommended time window Also Inform Applicant is done promptly 13 00 which is within the recommended 48 hours loan decision happened at 11 00 and inform applicant happened at 13 00 That is only two hours after decision so it is fine

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case ID 2 check sequence Application Received is at 2024 01 11 then Risk Assessment occurs at 08 20 and later Credit Check at 09 00 So clearly the credit check has occurred after risk assessment in case 2 The normative rule says Credit Check must occur before Risk Assessment So that is a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also subsequent events Loan Decision Rejected at 9 30 Then Finalize Documentation is performed at 10 00 which is not allowed for a rejection And then close case at 10 30 So two normative violations in case 2

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case ID 3 check sequence Application Received at Jan 12 09 00 Credit Check happened on Jan 14 10 00 That violates the desiderata timing of credit check should be done within 24 hours after application received but that is only a recommended guideline and not required by normative rules For case 3 credit check occurs on Jan 14 at 10 00 and then risk assessment is done on Jan 14 at 11 00 So they are still in the right order normative rule is followed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Loan Decision Approved at Jan 14 12 00 Then Inform Applicant occurred on Jan 16 15 00 For case 3 Loan Decision was on Jan 14 at 12 00 Inform Applicant occurred on Jan 16 at 15 00 which is roughly 2 days and 3 hours after decision So this is a desideratum violation suboptimal but not severe

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then finalization of documentation is done on Jan 17 09 00 and then close case on Jan 17 10 00 that is fine sequence wise And follow up call No Follow up Call for Case 3 so that is a recommended desiderata violation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now summarizing Case 1 Normative rules followed correctly no normative violations Case 2 Violates normative rules by having Risk Assessment occur before Credit Check and by finalizing documentation after loan decision that was rejected Case 3 Normative rules are adhered strictly as order is maintained

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now for desiderata Case 1 all recommended best practices were met except follow up call but it is optional Case 2 no timing issue and no desiderata issue beyond the strict violations already identified Case 3 desiderata are not met because Credit Check happened more than 24 hours after application was received Inform Applicant occurred later than the recommended time window and no follow up call was performed

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: For Case 2 the order violation Risk Assessment before Credit Check might be due to a process oversight or miscommunication The finalization of documentation after rejection could be an administrative error where documentation was processed despite the rejection decision

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: For Case 3 although sequence is maintained the delay in performing credit check and informing the applicant may be due to external factors such as resource constraints delays from third party credit bureaus or operational capacity issues The absence of a follow up call might indicate that this optional step was simply skipped
